scores.metric: sandbox --disable all (masked Levenshtein) — UNRELIABLE here: the register swap is masked to 0, so the sandbox cannot see the real gap. Full SHA1 is the only truth.

sessions.lever: diagnosis only — identified the two coupled cheats; no floor reduction (the real gap is masked)

full analysis + empirical confirmation. (1) RA: GCC priority formula from global.c:604 = floor_log2(n_refs)*n_refs/live_length*10k. result(n=6,len=400)=300 > sel2(n=3,len=124)=242 → result takes $s2. Empirically: pins removed + regfix kept = SHA1 46e36200 (RA mismatch). (2) Cross-jump: minimum-2 fires when case9/11 uses sel=0xD (both blocks `li s0,13; j sel_dispatch`). Mixed-exit-forms lever not applicable (neither path can return mid-function). No pure-C lever found for either issue.

reviewer.evidence: Two coupled forbidden cheats: (1) register pins result asm("s3")/sel2 asm("s2"); (2) regfix subst 'addiu $16,$zero,12'->'13' @149 (commented 'anti-cross-jump fix'). Removing pins flips RA (masked-invisible); removing the rule via setting case9/11 sel=0xD FAILS the full build. Likely needs cross-jump-defeating restructure + a pure-C RA lever, or canonical-asm auth. Session 2 (2026-06-16): exhaustive analysis confirms BOTH issues are plateaus — no pure-C lever exists for either.

remaining_gap.summary: 402-insn dispatcher. Target: result in $s3, sel2 in $s2. Without pins: result→$s2, sel2→$s3 (SHA1 46e36200). Without regfix (case9/11 sel=0xD): cross_jump merges ea0+fc0 blocks (SHA1 82e50f76). GCC priority formula (global.c:604) shows result (priority≈300) > sel2 (priority≈242): sel2 needs n_refs≥4 to win, but any 4th ref changes the binary (oracle has li a1,-1 not move a1,s2). Cross_jump minimum-2 fires with sel=0xD for case9/11. No pure-C solution for either issue found after exhaustive analysis.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: HEAD byte-matches ONLY via 2 cheat-asm pins + 1 regfix subst. No pure-C form found after exhaustive analysis of RA priority formula and cross_jump mechanism.

next_hypotheses: Adjudicator decision: (a) any pure-C RA lever to flip sel2>result without changing binary? (b) any structural approach to prevent cross_jump merge while both blocks emit 13? (c) park?

rejected_forms.reason: WRONG: full build SHA1 mismatch 82e50f76. Cross_jump fires when both case9/11 and D_800A3207==3 non-10 have sel=0xD — minimum-2 (`li s0,13; j sel_dispatch`) met → blocks merged → different binary.
Masked-sandbox 11->9 noise; RA swap persisted. Tiebreaker (allocno order) doesn't help since priorities differ (300 vs 242), not equal.
PROVEN this session: SHA1 46e36200 (RA mismatch). result→$s2 (wrong) without pins.

rejected_forms.form: func_8006BEC4(0xA, sel2) instead of func_8006BEC4(0xA, -1) at early exit
rejected_forms.reason: Would add 4th ref to sel2 (raising priority to 645 > result's 300), but changes binary: oracle has `li a1, -1` at dcc NOT `move a1, s2`. Cannot use this lever.
